topic: A technician performing a 'Three-Point Bend' test on a carbon fiber sample.
raw_generation:
{
  "script": "Scene 1 - Opening title: Three-Point Bend Test on a Carbon Fiber Sample.
The camera slowly surveys a carbon fiber coupon and a precision universal testing machine.

Scene 2 - Safety and PPE.
The technician wears safety glasses, gloves, and a lab coat. A brief on-screen safety reminder appears: always follow lab safety procedures.

Scene 3 - Setup.
The coupon is positioned on the lower supports of the three-point bending fixture. The center loading nose rests above the midpoint of the span.

Scene 4 - Alignment and configuration.
The technician adjusts the span length and aligns the coupon so the load will be applied at the center. A digital readout shows span length and calibration status.

Scene 5 - Pre-test checks.
A close-up shows the fiber weave, the coupon ends clamped, and the alignment verified with a crosshair. The operator confirms there is no looseness and the fixture is secured.

Scene 6 - Test start.
The machine begins to apply load at a controlled rate. The load and deflection are displayed on the monitor as the carbon fiber begins to bend.

Scene 7 - Test progress and data capture.
As load increases, the graph updates and the operator saves the data to the computer. The camera shows P vs delta on the screen.

Scene 8 - Post-test and results.
The machine stops. The coupon is removed for inspection. A typical failure mode in carbon fiber samples is shown by a clean fracture along the fiber, or matrix cracking near the surface. The voiceover notes how peak load is used to compute flexural strength.

Scene 9 - Calculation and interpretation.
A quick graphic shows the flexural strength formula, and a note explains that the exact equation may differ by specimen geometry. The narrator mentions using the standard equation sigma equals 3 P L divided by 2 b d squared for a rectangular cross-section, or manufacturer guidance.

Scene 10 - Closing.
A wrap-up slide summarizes the purpose of the test and its applications in material selection and quality control.",
  "narration": "In this video we demonstrate a three point bend test on a carbon fiber coupon to evaluate its flexural strength and stiffness. A technician correctly sets up the fixture, aligns the specimen, and runs a controlled bend until the drop or fracture occurs. First, the carbon fiber coupon is placed on the two lower supports with the center loading nose positioned at the midpoint. Safety checks are performed and the operator wears appropriate PPE. The span length is set and confirmed on the digital readout. The coupon is checked for proper orientation and clamped securely before the test begins. The machine applies load at a controlled rate, and the load and deflection are monitored in real time on the screen. The display captures peak load, and when the test ends, the data is saved for analysis. After removal, the specimen is inspected to observe typical carbon fiber failure modes such as fiber fracture or matrix cracking. The peak load is used to calculate flexural strength according to the standard equation for the specimen geometry, with variations depending on the cross-section. A quick summary graphic shows the key results: flexural strength, stiffness, and potential quality indicators. This demonstration highlights how three point bending helps engineers quantify the bending performance of carbon fiber composites for aerospace, automotive, and sport equipment applications. Remember to follow all lab safety procedures and consult the equipment manual for exact testing parameters."
}

parsed_generation.script:
Scene 1 - Opening title: Three-Point Bend Test on a Carbon Fiber Sample.
The camera slowly surveys a carbon fiber coupon and a precision universal testing machine.

Scene 2 - Safety and PPE.
The technician wears safety glasses, gloves, and a lab coat. A brief on-screen safety reminder appears: always follow lab safety procedures.

Scene 3 - Setup.
The coupon is positioned on the lower supports of the three-point bending fixture. The center loading nose rests above the midpoint of the span.

Scene 4 - Alignment and configuration.
The technician adjusts the span length and aligns the coupon so the load will be applied at the center. A digital readout shows span length and calibration status.

Scene 5 - Pre-test checks.
A close-up shows the fiber weave, the coupon ends clamped, and the alignment verified with a crosshair. The operator confirms there is no looseness and the fixture is secured.

Scene 6 - Test start.
The machine begins to apply load at a controlled rate. The load and deflection are displayed on the monitor as the carbon fiber begins to bend.

Scene 7 - Test progress and data capture.
As load increases, the graph updates and the operator saves the data to the computer. The camera shows P vs delta on the screen.

Scene 8 - Post-test and results.
The machine stops. The coupon is removed for inspection. A typical failure mode in carbon fiber samples is shown by a clean fracture along the fiber, or matrix cracking near the surface. The voiceover notes how peak load is used to compute flexural strength.

Scene 9 - Calculation and interpretation.
A quick graphic shows the flexural strength formula, and a note explains that the exact equation may differ by specimen geometry. The narrator mentions using the standard equation sigma equals 3 P L divided by 2 b d squared for a rectangular cross-section, or manufacturer guidance.

Scene 10 - Closing.
A wrap-up slide summarizes the purpose of the test and its applications in material selection and quality control.
parsed_generation.narration: In this video we demonstrate a three point bend test on a carbon fiber coupon to evaluate its flexural strength and stiffness. A technician correctly sets up the fixture, aligns the specimen, and runs a controlled bend until the drop or fracture occurs. First, the carbon fiber coupon is placed on the two lower supports with the center loading nose positioned at the midpoint. Safety checks are performed and the operator wears appropriate PPE. The span length is set and confirmed on the digital readout. The coupon is checked for proper orientation and clamped securely before the test begins. The machine applies load at a controlled rate, and the load and deflection are monitored in real time on the screen. The display captures peak load, and when the test ends, the data is saved for analysis. After removal, the specimen is inspected to observe typical carbon fiber failure modes such as fiber fracture or matrix cracking. The peak load is used to calculate flexural strength according to the standard equation for the specimen geometry, with variations depending on the cross-section. A quick summary graphic shows the key results: flexural strength, stiffness, and potential quality indicators. This demonstration highlights how three point bending helps engineers quantify the bending performance of carbon fiber composites for aerospace, automotive, and sport equipment applications. Remember to follow all lab safety procedures and consult the equipment manual for exact testing parameters.

feedback:
The output covers the three-point bend test on a carbon fiber coupon and includes key test stages, but it lacks timestamped shot segments required by the gold standard. To align with the reference, structure the script with [00:00-00:04] style timestamps, define precise shot angles (wide, close-up, macro, tracking), and keep action-first language. Also, trim extraneous elements and ensure concise, cinema-ready descriptions that map one shot to one timestamp, focusing on the technician setup, load application, and fracture outcomes.
Failed checks: example_alignment
strengths: Covers major test stages (setup, load, data capture, post-test inspection)
fixes: Add explicit timestamped shot segments in the script
Use definitive camera-angle cues (wide, close-up, macro, tracking) and map actions to timestamps
